definitions.text: To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.
examples: I was sitting on the sofa for a long time, feeling too lazy to move.

definitions.text: To act; to take action; to begin to act
examples: Come on guys, let's move: there's work to do!

definitions.text: The act of moving; a movement.
examples: A slight move of the tiller, and the boat will go off course.
The drummer Cynthia praised her best friends' dance moves to the music.

definitions.text: An act for the attainment of an object; a step in the execution of a plan or purpose.
examples: He made another move towards becoming a naturalized citizen.

definitions.text: A formalized or practiced action used in athletics, dance, physical exercise, self-defense, hand-to-hand combat, etc.
examples: She always gets spontaneous applause for that one move.
He can win a match with that one move.
